name: SIGMA - Potential NetWire RAT Activity - Registry
author: Christopher Peacock
description: Detects registry keys related to NetWire RAT
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"CreateKey" and TargetObject:"*\\software\\NetWire*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via New AMSI Providers - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker registers a new AMSI provider in order to achieve persistence
false_positives: Legitimate security products adding their own AMSI providers. Filter these according to your environment
query: any where EventID:12 and ((EventType:"CreateKey" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\AMSI\\Providers\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\AMSI\\Providers\\*"))) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential COM Object Hijacking Via TreatAs Subkey - Registry
author: Kutepov Anton, oscd.community
description: Detects COM object hijacking via TreatAs subkey
false_positives: Maybe some system utilities in rare cases use linking keys for backward compatibility
query: any where EventID:12 and ((EventType:"CreateKey" and (TargetObject:"*HKU\\*" and TargetObject:"*Classes\\CLSID\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\TreatAs*")) and (not Image:"C:\\WINDOWS\\system32\\svchost.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Disk Cleanup Handler - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects when an attacker modifies values of the Disk Cleanup Handler in the registry to achieve persistence.
The disk cleanup manager is part of the operating system. It displays the dialog box […]
The user has the option of enabling or disabling individual handlers by selecting or clearing their check box in the disk cleanup manager's UI.
Although Windows comes with a number of disk cleanup handlers, they aren't designed to handle files produced by other applications.
Instead, the disk cleanup manager is designed to be flexible and extensible by enabling any developer to implement and register their own disk cleanup handler.
Any developer can extend the available disk cleanup services by implementing and registering a disk cleanup handler.
false_positives: Legitimate new entry added by windows
query: any where EventID:12 and ((EventType:"CreateKey" and TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\VolumeCaches\\*") and (not (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Active Setup Temp Folders", "*\\BranchCache", "*\\Content Indexer Cleaner", "*\\D3D Shader Cache", "*\\Delivery Optimization Files", "*\\Device Driver Packages", "*\\Diagnostic Data Viewer database files", "*\\Downloaded Program Files", "*\\DownloadsFolder", "*\\Feedback Hub Archive log files", "*\\Internet Cache Files", "*\\Language Pack", "*\\Microsoft Office Temp Files", "*\\Offline Pages Files", "*\\Old ChkDsk Files", "*\\Previous Installations", "*\\Recycle Bin", "*\\RetailDemo Offline Content", "*\\Setup Log Files", "*\\System error memory dump files", "*\\System error minidump files", "*\\Temporary Files", "*\\Temporary Setup Files", "*\\Temporary Sync Files", "*\\Thumbnail Cache", "*\\Update Cleanup", "*\\Upgrade Discarded Files", "*\\User file versions", "*\\Windows Defender", "*\\Windows Error Reporting Files", "*\\Windows ESD installation files", "*\\Windows Upgrade Log Files"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Logon Scripts - Registry
author: Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure)
description: Detects creation of "UserInitMprLogonScript" registry value which can be used as a persistence method by malicious actors
false_positives: Investigate the contents of the "UserInitMprLogonScript" value to determine of the added script is legitimate
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"CreateKey" and TargetObject:"*UserInitMprLogonScript*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Sysinternal Tool Execution - Registry
author: Markus Neis
description: Detects the execution of a Sysinternals Tool via the creation of the "accepteula" registry key
false_positives: Programs that use the same Registry Key
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"CreateKey" and TargetObject:"*\\EulaAccepted")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Execution Of Renamed Sysinternals Tools - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of the "accepteula" key related to the Sysinternals tools being created from executables with the wrong name (e.g. a renamed Sysinternals tool)
query: any where EventID:12 and ((EventType:"CreateKey" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Active Directory Explorer*", "*\\Handle*", "*\\LiveKd*", "*\\ProcDump*", "*\\Process Explorer*", "*\\PsExec*", "*\\PsLoggedon*", "*\\PsLoglist*", "*\\PsPasswd*", "*\\PsPing*", "*\\PsService*", "*\\SDelete*")) and TargetObject:"*\\EulaAccepted") and (not (Image like~ ("*\\ADExplorer.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64.exe", "*\\handle.exe", "*\\handle64.exe", "*\\livekd.exe", "*\\livekd64.exe", "*\\procdump.exe", "*\\procdump64.exe", "*\\procexp.exe", "*\\procexp64.exe", "*\\PsExec.exe", "*\\PsExec64.exe", "*\\PsLoggedon.exe", "*\\PsLoggedon64.exe", "*\\psloglist.exe", "*\\psloglist64.exe", "*\\pspasswd.exe", "*\\pspasswd64.exe", "*\\PsPing.exe", "*\\PsPing64.exe", "*\\PsService.exe", "*\\PsService64.exe", "*\\sdelete.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Sysinternals Tools Execution - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of some potentially unwanted tools such as PsExec, Procdump, etc. (part of the Sysinternals suite) via the creation of the "accepteula" registry key.
false_positives: Legitimate use of SysInternals tools. Filter the legitimate paths used in your environment
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"CreateKey" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Active Directory Explorer*", "*\\Handle*", "*\\LiveKd*", "*\\Process Explorer*", "*\\ProcDump*", "*\\PsExec*", "*\\PsLoglist*", "*\\PsPasswd*", "*\\SDelete*", "*\\Sysinternals*")) and TargetObject:"*\\EulaAccepted")

name: SIGMA - Delete Defender Scan ShellEx Context Menu Registry Key
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description: Detects deletion of registry key that adds 'Scan with Defender' option in context menu. Attackers may use this to make it harder for users to scan files that are suspicious.
false_positives: Unlikely as this weakens defenses and normally would not be done even if using another AV.
query: any where EventID:12 and (TargetObject:"*shellex\\ContextMenuHandlers\\EPP*" and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Defender\\*")) and Image:"*\\MsMpEng.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Windows Recall Feature Enabled - DisableAIDataAnalysis Value Deleted
author: Sajid Nawaz Khan
description:
Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature via registry manipulation. Windows Recall can be enabled by deleting the existing "DisableAIDataAnalysis" registry value.
Adversaries may enable Windows Recall as part of post-exploitation discovery and collection activities.
This rule assumes that Recall is already explicitly disabled on the host, and subsequently enabled by the adversary.
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"DeleteValue" and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\WindowsAI\\DisableAIDataAnalysis")

name: SIGMA - Folder Removed From Exploit Guard ProtectedFolders List - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the removal of folders from the "ProtectedFolders" list of of exploit guard. This could indicate an attacker trying to launch an encryption process or trying to manipulate data inside of the protected folder
false_positives: Legitimate administrators removing applications (should always be investigated)
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"DeleteValue" and TargetObject:"*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Windows Defender Exploit Guard\\Controlled Folder Access\\ProtectedFolders*")

name: SIGMA - Terminal Server Client Connection History Cleared - Registry
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the deletion of registry keys containing the MSTSC connection history
query: any where EventID:12 and ((EventType:"DeleteValue" and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Terminal Server Client\\Default\\MRU*") or (EventType:"DeleteKey" and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Terminal Server Client\\Servers\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Removal Of AMSI Provider Registry Keys
author: frack113
description: Detects the deletion of AMSI provider registry key entries in HKLM\Software\Microsoft\AMSI. This technique could be used by an attacker in order to disable AMSI inspection.
query: any where EventID:12 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*{2781761E-28E0-4109-99FE-B9D127C57AFE}", "*{A7C452EF-8E9F-42EB-9F2B-245613CA0DC9}")) and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Defender\\*")) and Image:"*\\MsMpEng.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Removal of Potential COM Hijacking Registry Keys
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description:
Detects any deletion of entries in ".*\shell\open\command" registry keys.
These registry keys might have been used for COM hijacking activities by a threat actor or an attacker and the deletion could indicate steps to remove its tracks.
false_positives: Legitimate software (un)installations are known to cause false positives. Please add them as a filter when encountered
query: any where EventID:12 and (TargetObject:"*\\shell\\open\\command" and (not (Image:"*C:\\Windows\\explorer.exe" or Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\svchost.exe" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\msiexec.exe", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\msiexec.exe")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*")) or Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenWith.exe")) and (not ((Image:"*\\Dropbox.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Dropbox.*") or (Image:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\Wireshark_uninstaller.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\wireshark-capture-file\\*") or (Image:"*peazip*" and TargetObject:"*\\PeaZip.*") or (Image:"*\\Everything.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Everything.*") or Image:"C:\\Windows\\Installer\\MSI*" or (Image:"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Java\\*" and Image:"*\\installer.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Classes\\WOW6432Node\\CLSID\\{4299124F-F2C3-41b4-9C73-9236B2AD0E8F}*") or Image:"*\\Microsoft\\EdgeUpdate\\Install*" or ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avira\\Antivirus\\", "C:\\Program Files\\Avira\\Antivirus\\")) and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\CLSID\\{305CA226-D286-468e-B848-2B2E8E697B74}\\Shell\\Open\\Command", "*\\AntiVir.Keyfile\\shell\\open\\command"))) or ((Image:"*AppData\\Local\\Temp*" and Image:"*\\setup.exe*") or (Image:"*\\Temp\\is-*" and Image:"*\\target.tmp*")) or Image:"*\\ninite.exe" or (Image:"*\\reg.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Discord\\shell\\open\\command") or (Image:"*\\Spotify.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Spotify\\shell\\open\\command") or (Image:"*C:\\eclipse\\eclipse.exe" and TargetObject:"*_Classes\\eclipse+*") or (Image:"*\\Temp*" and Image:"*\\TeamViewer*"))))

name: SIGMA - RunMRU Registry Key Deletion - Registry
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects attempts to delete the RunMRU registry key, which stores the history of commands executed via the run dialog.
In the clickfix techniques, the phishing lures instruct users to open a run dialog through (Win + R) and execute malicious commands.
Adversaries may delete this key to cover their tracks after executing commands.
query: any where EventID:12 and TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\RunMRU"

name: SIGMA - Removal Of Index Value to Hide Schedule Task - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when the "index" value of a scheduled task is removed or deleted from the registry. Which effectively hides it from any tooling such as "schtasks /query"
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"DeleteKey" and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Schedule\\TaskCache\\Tree\\*" and TargetObject:"*Index*"))

name: SIGMA - Removal Of SD Value to Hide Schedule Task - Registry
author: Sittikorn S
description: Remove SD (Security Descriptor) value in \Schedule\TaskCache\Tree registry hive to hide schedule task. This technique is used by Tarrask malware
query: any where EventID:12 and (EventType:"DeleteKey" and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Schedule\\TaskCache\\Tree\\*" and TargetObject:"*SD*"))

name: SIGMA - Creation of a Local Hidden User Account by Registry
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Sysmon registry detection of a local hidden user account.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (TargetObject:"*\\SAM\\SAM\\Domains\\Account\\Users\\Names\\*" and TargetObject:"*$" and Image:"*\\lsass.exe")

name: SIGMA - Pandemic Registry Key
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Pandemic Windows Implant
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\services\\null\\Instance*"

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Via Wsreset
author: oscd.community, Dmitry Uchakin
description: Unfixed method for UAC bypass from Windows 10. WSReset.exe file associated with the Windows Store. It will run a binary file contained in a low-privilege registry.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\AppX82a6gwre4fdg3bt635tn5ctqjf8msdd2\\Shell\\open\\command"

name: SIGMA - CMSTP Execution Registry Event
author: Nik Seetharaman
description: Detects various indicators of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer execution
false_positives: Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\cmmgr32.exe*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Threat Severity Default Action Modified
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description:
Detects modifications or creations of Windows Defender's default threat action settings based on severity to 'allow' or take 'no action'.
This is a highly suspicious configuration change that effectively disables Defender's ability to automatically mitigate threats of a certain severity level,
allowing malicious software to run unimpeded. An attacker might use this technique to bypass defenses before executing payloads.
false_positives: Legitimate administration via scripts or tools (e.g., SCCM, Intune, GPO enforcement). Correlate with administrative activity.
Software installations that legitimately modify Defender settings (less common for these specific keys).
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Threats\\ThreatSeverityDefaultAction\\*" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\1", "*\\2", "*\\4", "*\\5")) and (Details like~ ("DWORD (0x00000006)", "DWORD (0x00000009)")))

name: SIGMA - Disable Security Events Logging Adding Reg Key MiniNt
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community
description: Detects the addition of a key 'MiniNt' to the registry. Upon a reboot, Windows Event Log service will stop writing events.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject:"HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt" and EventType:"CreateKey") or NewName:"HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt")

name: SIGMA - Wdigest CredGuard Registry Modification
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description:
Detects potential malicious modification of the property value of IsCredGuardEnabled from
HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest to disable Cred Guard on a system.
This is usually used with UseLogonCredential to manipulate the caching credentials.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\IsCredGuardEnabled"

name: SIGMA - Esentutl Volume Shadow Copy Service Keys
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Detects the volume shadow copy service initialization and processing via esentutl. Registry keys such as HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\VSS\\Diag\\VolSnap\\Volume are captured.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject:"*System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\VSS*" and Image:"*esentutl.exe") and (not TargetObject:"*System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\VSS\\Start*"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Credential Editor Registry
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE)
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*Services\\WCESERVICE\\Start*"

name: SIGMA - HybridConnectionManager Service Installation - Registry
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Detects the installation of the Azure Hybrid Connection Manager service to allow remote code execution from Azure function.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (TargetObject:"*\\Services\\HybridConnectionManager*" or (EventType:"SetValue" and Details:"*Microsoft.HybridConnectionManager.Listener.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Qakbot Registry Activity
author: Hieu Tran
description: Detects a registry key used by IceID in a campaign that distributes malicious OneNote files
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\Software\\firm\\soft\\Name"

name: SIGMA - Registry Entries For Azorult Malware
author: Trent Liffick
description: Detects the presence of a registry key created during Azorult execution
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((EventID like~ (12, 13)) and TargetObject:"*SYSTEM\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\services\\localNETService")

name: SIGMA - PrinterNightmare Mimikatz Driver Name
author: Markus Neis, @markus_neis, Florian Roth
description: Detects static QMS 810 and mimikatz driver name used by Mimikatz as exploited in CVE-2021-1675 and CVE-2021-34527
false_positives: Legitimate installation of printer driver QMS 810, Texas Instruments microLaser printer (unlikely)
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Control\\Print\\Environments\\Windows x64\\Drivers\\Version-3\\QMS 810\\*", "*\\Control\\Print\\Environments\\Windows x64\\Drivers\\Version-3\\mimikatz*")) or (TargetObject:"*legitprinter*" and TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Print\\Environments\\Windows*") or ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Control\\Print\\Environments*", "*\\CurrentVersion\\Print\\Printers*")) and (TargetObject like~ ("*Gentil Kiwi*", "*mimikatz printer*", "*Kiwi Legit Printer*"))))

name: SIGMA - Path To Screensaver Binary Modified
author: Bartlomiej Czyz @bczyz1, oscd.community
description: Detects value modification of registry key containing path to binary used as screensaver.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (TargetObject:"*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\SCRNSAVE.EXE" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\explorer.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Narrator's Feedback-Hub Persistence
author: Dmitriy Lifanov, oscd.community
description: Detects abusing Windows 10 Narrator's Feedback-Hub
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((EventType:"DeleteValue" and TargetObject:"*\\AppXypsaf9f1qserqevf0sws76dx4k9a5206\\Shell\\open\\command\\DelegateExecute") or TargetObject:"*\\AppXypsaf9f1qserqevf0sws76dx4k9a5206\\Shell\\open\\command\\(Default)")

name: SIGMA - NetNTLM Downgrade Attack - Registry
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), wagga, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Splunk STRT)
description: Detects NetNTLM downgrade attack
false_positives: Services or tools that set the values to more restrictive values
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject:"*SYSTEM\\*" and TargetObject:"*ControlSet*" and TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Lsa*") and ((TargetObject:"*\\lmcompatibilitylevel" and (Details like~ ("DWORD (0x00000000)", "DWORD (0x00000001)", "DWORD (0x00000002)"))) or (TargetObject:"*\\NtlmMinClientSec" and (Details like~ ("DWORD (0x00000000)", "DWORD (0x00000010)", "DWORD (0x00000020)", "DWORD (0x00000030)"))) or TargetObject:"*\\RestrictSendingNTLMTraffic"))

name: SIGMA - New DLL Added to AppCertDlls Registry Key
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community
description:
Dynamic-link libraries (DLLs) that are specified in the AppCertDLLs value in the Registry key can be abused to obtain persistence and privilege escalation
by causing a malicious DLL to be loaded and run in the context of separate processes on the computer.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (TargetObject:"HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\AppCertDlls" or NewName:"HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\AppCertDlls")

name: SIGMA - New DLL Added to AppInit_DLLs Registry Key
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community, Tim Shelton
description: DLLs that are specified in the AppInit_DLLs value in the Registry key HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Windows are loaded by user32.dll into every process that loads user32.dll
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (((TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows\\AppInit_Dlls", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Wow6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows\\AppInit_Dlls")) or (NewName like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows\\AppInit_Dlls", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Wow6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows\\AppInit_Dlls"))) and (not Details:"(Empty)"))

name: SIGMA - Office Application Startup - Office Test
author: omkar72
description: Detects the addition of office test registry that allows a user to specify an arbitrary DLL that will be executed every time an Office application is started
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Office test\\Special\\Perf*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Registry Trust Record Modification
author: Antonlovesdnb, Trent Liffick (@tliffick)
description: Alerts on trust record modification within the registry, indicating usage of macros
false_positives: This will alert on legitimate macro usage as well, additional tuning is required
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\Security\\Trusted Documents\\TrustRecords*"

name: SIGMA - Registry Persistence Mechanisms in Recycle Bin
author: frack113
description: Detects persistence registry keys for Recycle Bin
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((EventType:"RenameKey" and NewName:"*\\CLSID\\{645FF040-5081-101B-9F08-00AA002F954E}\\shell\\open*") or (EventType:"SetValue" and TargetObject:"*\\CLSID\\{645FF040-5081-101B-9F08-00AA002F954E}\\shell\\open\\command\\(Default)*"))

name: SIGMA - New PortProxy Registry Entry Added
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Detects the modification of the PortProxy registry key which is used for port forwarding.
false_positives: WSL2 network bridge PowerShell script used for WSL/Kubernetes/Docker (e.g. https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/4150#issuecomment-504209723)
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*\\Services\\PortProxy\\v4tov4\\tcp\\*"

name: SIGMA - RedMimicry Winnti Playbook Registry Manipulation
author: Alexander Rausch
description: Detects actions caused by the RedMimicry Winnti playbook
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\HTMLHelp\\data*"

name: SIGMA - WINEKEY Registry Modification
author: omkar72
description: Detects potential malicious modification of run keys by winekey or team9 backdoor
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\Backup Mgr"

name: SIGMA - Run Once Task Configuration in Registry
author: Avneet Singh @v3t0_, oscd.community
description: Rule to detect the configuration of Run Once registry key. Configured payload can be run by runonce.exe /AlternateShellStartup
false_positives: Legitimate modification of the registry key by legitimate program
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Active Setup\\Installed Components*" and TargetObject:"*\\StubPath") and (not ((Details:"*C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\*" and Details:"*\\Installer\\chrmstp.exe\" --configure-user-settings --verbose-logging --system-level*") or ((Details like~ ("*C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\*", "*C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\*")) and Details:"*\\Installer\\setup.exe\" --configure-user-settings --verbose-logging --system-level --msedge --channel=stable"))))

name: SIGMA - Shell Open Registry Keys Manipulation
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the shell open key manipulation (exefile and ms-settings) used for persistence and the pattern of UAC Bypass using fodhelper.exe, computerdefaults.exe, slui.exe via registry keys (e.g. UACMe 33 or 62)
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((EventType:"SetValue" and TargetObject:"*Classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command\\SymbolicLinkValue" and Details:"*\\Software\\Classes\\{*") or TargetObject:"*Classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command\\DelegateExecute" or ((EventType:"SetValue" and (TargetObject like~ ("*Classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command\\(Default)", "*Classes\\exefile\\shell\\open\\command\\(Default)"))) and (not Details:"(Empty)")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS SilentProcessExit Technique
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the Registry in which a monitor program gets registered to dump the memory of the lsass.exe process
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and TargetObject:"*Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\SilentProcessExit\\lsass.exe*"

name: SIGMA - Security Support Provider (SSP) Added to LSA Configuration
author: iwillkeepwatch
description: Detects the addition of a SSP to the registry. Upon a reboot or API call, SSP DLLs gain access to encrypted and plaintext passwords stored in Windows.
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Control\\Lsa\\Security Packages", "*\\Control\\Lsa\\OSConfig\\Security Packages")) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\system32\\msiexec.exe", "C:\\Windows\\syswow64\\MsiExec.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Sticky Key Like Backdoor Usage - Registry
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), @twjackomo, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects the usage and installation of a backdoor that uses an option to register a malicious debugger for built-in tools that are accessible in the login screen
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\sethc.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\utilman.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\osk.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\Magnify.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\Narrator.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\DisplaySwitch.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\atbroker.exe\\Debugger", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\HelpPane.exe\\Debugger"))

name: SIGMA - Atbroker Registry Change
author: Mateusz Wydra, oscd.community
description: Detects creation/modification of Assistive Technology applications and persistence with usage of 'at'
false_positives: Creation of non-default, legitimate at usage
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject like~ ("*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Accessibility\\ATs*", "*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Accessibility\\Configuration*")) and (not ((Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\atbroker.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Accessibility\\Configuration*" and Details:"(Empty)") or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\Installer\\MSI*" and TargetObject:"*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Accessibility\\ATs*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Run Key from Download
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poude (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the suspicious RUN keys created by software located in Download or temporary Outlook/Internet Explorer directories
false_positives: Software installers downloaded and used by users
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((Image like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Packages\\Microsoft.Outlook_*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Olk\\Attachments\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet Files\\Content.Outlook\\*", "*\\Local Settings\\Temporary Internet Files\\*")) and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run*")))

name: SIGMA - DLL Load via LSASS
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a method to load DLL via LSASS process using an undocumented Registry key
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\NTDS\\DirectoryServiceExtPt*", "*\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\NTDS\\LsaDbExtPt*")) and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\lsass.exe" and (Details like~ ("%%systemroot%%\\system32\\ntdsa.dll", "%%systemroot%%\\system32\\lsadb.dll")))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Camera and Microphone Access
author: Den Iuzvyk
description: Detects Processes accessing the camera and microphone from suspicious folder
false_positives: Unlikely, there could be conferencing software running from a Temp folder accessing the devices
query: any where (EventID like~ (12, 13, 14)) and ((TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\CapabilityAccessManager\\ConsentStore\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\NonPackaged*") and (TargetObject like~ ("*microphone*", "*webcam*")) and (TargetObject like~ ("*:#Windows#Temp#*", "*:#$Recycle.bin#*", "*:#Temp#*", "*:#Users#Public#*", "*:#Users#Default#*", "*:#Users#Desktop#*")))

name: SIGMA - Enable Remote Connection Between Anonymous Computer - AllowAnonymousCallback
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects enabling of the "AllowAnonymousCallback" registry value, which allows a remote connection between computers that do not have a trust relationship.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\WBEM\\CIMOM\\AllowAnonymousCallback*" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Registry Persistence via Service in Safe Mode
author: frack113
description: Detects the modification of the registry to allow a driver or service to persist in Safe Mode.
query: any where EventID:13 and (((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Minimal\\*", "*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Network\\*")) and TargetObject:"*\\(Default)" and Details:"Service") and (not ((Image:"C:\\WINDOWS\\system32\\msiexec.exe" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Minimal\\SAVService\\(Default)", "*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Network\\SAVService\\(Default)"))) or (Image:"*\\MBAMInstallerService.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\MBAMService\\(Default)" and Details:"Service") or (Image:"C:\\Hexnode\\Hexnode Agent\\Current\\HexnodeAgent.exe" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Minimal\\Hexnode Updater\\(Default)", "*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Network\\Hexnode Updater\\(Default)", "*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Minimal\\Hexnode Agent\\(Default)", "*\\Control\\SafeBoot\\Network\\Hexnode Agent\\(Default)")) and Details:"Service"))))

name: SIGMA - Add Port Monitor Persistence in Registry
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may use port monitors to run an attacker supplied DLL during system boot for persistence or privilege escalation.
A port monitor can be set through the AddMonitor API call to set a DLL to be loaded at startup.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Print\\Monitors\\*" and Details:"*.dll") and (not ((Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\spoolsv.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Print\\Monitors\\CutePDF Writer Monitor v4.0\\Driver*" and Details:"cpwmon64_v40.dll" and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*"))) or TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Print\\Monitors\\MONVNC\\Driver*" or (TargetObject:"*Control\\Print\\Environments\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\Drivers\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\VNC Printer*"))))

name: SIGMA - Add Debugger Entry To AeDebug For Persistence
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker adds a new "Debugger" value to the "AeDebug" key in order to achieve persistence which will get invoked when an application crashes
false_positives: Legitimate use of the key to setup a debugger. Which is often the case on developers machines
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\AeDebug\\Debugger*" and Details:"*.dll") and (not Details:"\"C:\\WINDOWS\\system32\\vsjitdebugger.exe\" -p %ld -e %ld -j 0x%p"))

name: SIGMA - Allow RDP Remote Assistance Feature
author: frack113
description: Detect enable rdp feature to allow specific user to rdp connect on the targeted machine
false_positives: Legitimate use of the feature (alerts should be investigated either way)
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Terminal Server\\fAllowToGetHelp" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Potential AMSI COM Server Hijacking
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the AMSI come server registry key in order disable AMSI scanning functionalities. When AMSI attempts to starts its COM component, it will query its registered CLSID and return a non-existent COM server. This causes a load failure and prevents any scanning methods from being accessed, ultimately rendering AMSI useless
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\CLSID\\{fdb00e52-a214-4aa1-8fba-4357bb0072ec}\\InProcServer32\\(Default)" and (not Details:"%windir%\\system32\\amsi.dll"))

author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

name: SIGMA - New BgInfo.EXE Custom DB Path Registry Configuration
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects setting of a new registry database value related to BgInfo configuration. Attackers can for example set this value to save the results of the commands executed by BgInfo in order to exfiltrate information.
false_positives: Legitimate use of external DB to save the results
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Winternals\\BGInfo\\Database"

name: SIGMA - New BgInfo.EXE Custom VBScript Registry Configuration
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects setting of a new registry value related to BgInfo configuration, which can be abused to execute custom VBScript via "BgInfo.exe"
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Winternals\\BGInfo\\UserFields\\*" and Details:"4*")

name: SIGMA - New BgInfo.EXE Custom WMI Query Registry Configuration
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects setting of a new registry value related to BgInfo configuration, which can be abused to execute custom WMI query via "BgInfo.exe"
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Winternals\\BGInfo\\UserFields\\*" and Details:"6*")

name: SIGMA - Bypass UAC Using DelegateExecute
author: frack113
description: Bypasses User Account Control using a fileless method
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\open\\command\\DelegateExecute" and Details:"(Empty)")

name: SIGMA - Bypass UAC Using Event Viewer
author: frack113
description: Bypasses User Account Control using Event Viewer and a relevant Windows Registry modification
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*_Classes\\mscfile\\shell\\open\\command\\(Default)" and (not Details:"%SystemRoot%\\system32\\mmc.exe \"%1\" %*"))

name: SIGMA - Bypass UAC Using SilentCleanup Task
author: frack113, Nextron Systems
description:
Detects the setting of the environement variable "windir" to a non default value.
Attackers often abuse this variable in order to trigger a UAC bypass via the "SilentCleanup" task.
The SilentCleanup task located in %windir%\system32\cleanmgr.exe is an auto-elevated task that can be abused to elevate any file with administrator privileges without prompting UAC.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Environment\\windir" and (not Details:"%SystemRoot%"))

name: SIGMA - Default RDP Port Changed to Non Standard Port
author: frack113
description:
Detects changes to the default RDP port.
Remote desktop is a common feature in operating systems. It allows a user to log into a remote system using an interactive session with a graphical user interface.
Microsoft refers to its implementation of the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) as Remote Desktop Services (RDS).
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Terminal Server\\WinStations\\RDP-Tcp\\PortNumber" and (not Details:"DWORD (0x00000d3d)"))

name: SIGMA - IE Change Domain Zone
author: frack113
description: Hides the file extension through modification of the registry
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Internet Settings\\ZoneMap\\Domains\\*" and (not (Details like~ ("DWORD (0x00000000)", "DWORD (0x00000001)", "(Empty)"))))

name: SIGMA - Sysmon Driver Altitude Change
author: B.Talebi
description:
Detects changes in Sysmon driver altitude value.
If the Sysmon driver is configured to load at an altitude of another registered service, it will fail to load at boot.
false_positives: Legitimate driver altitude change to hide sysmon
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Services\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\Instances\\Sysmon Instance\\Altitude")

name: SIGMA - Change Winevt Channel Access Permission Via Registry
author: frack113
description: Detects tampering with the "ChannelAccess" registry key in order to change access to Windows event channel.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\ChannelAccess" and (Details like~ ("*(A;;0x1;;;LA)*", "*(A;;0x1;;;SY)*", "*(A;;0x5;;;BA)*"))) and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\servicing\\TrustedInstaller.exe" or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*" and Image:"*\\TiWorker.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - ClickOnce Trust Prompt Tampering
author: @SerkinValery, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the ClickOnce trust prompt registry key in order to enable an installation from different locations such as the Internet.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\MICROSOFT\\.NETFramework\\Security\\TrustManager\\PromptingLevel\\*" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Internet", "*\\LocalIntranet", "*\\MyComputer", "*\\TrustedSites", "*\\UntrustedSites")) and Details:"Enabled")

name: SIGMA - Potential CobaltStrike Service Installations - Registry
author: Wojciech Lesicki
description: Detects known malicious service installs that appear in cases in which a Cobalt Strike beacon elevates privileges or lateral movement.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services*" or (TargetObject:"*\\System\\ControlSet*" and TargetObject:"*\\Services*")) and ((Details:"*ADMIN$*" and Details:"*.exe*") or (Details:"*%COMSPEC%*" and Details:"*start*" and Details:"*powershell*")))

name: SIGMA - COM Hijack via Sdclt
author: Omkar Gudhate
description: Detects changes to 'HKCU\Software\Classes\Folder\shell\open\command\DelegateExecute'
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Classes\\Folder\\shell\\open\\command\\DelegateExecute*"

name: SIGMA - CrashControl CrashDump Disabled
author: Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects disabling the CrashDump per registry (as used by HermeticWiper)
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\CrashControl*" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Security Event Logging Disabled via MiniNt Registry Key - Registry Set
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the addition of the 'MiniNt' key to the registry. Upon a reboot, Windows Event Log service will stop writing events.
Windows Event Log is a service that collects and stores event logs from the operating system and applications. It is an important component of Windows security and auditing.
Adversary may want to disable this service to disable logging of security events which could be used to detect their activities.
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt\\(Default)"

name: SIGMA - Service Binary in Suspicious Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detect the creation of a service with a service binary located in a suspicious directory
query: any where EventID:13 and (((TargetObject:"HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\Start" and (Image like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Perflogs\\*", "*\\ADMIN$\\*", "*\\Temp\\*")) and (Details like~ ("DWORD (0x00000000)", "DWORD (0x00000001)", "DWORD (0x00000002)"))) or (TargetObject:"HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\ImagePath" and (Details like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Perflogs\\*", "*\\ADMIN$\\*", "*\\Temp\\*")))) and (not ((Image:"*\\Common Files\\*" and Image:"*\\Temp\\*") or (TargetObject:"*\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\MBAMInstallerService\\ImagePath" and Details:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\MBAMInstallerService.exe\"" and Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\services.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Custom File Open Handler Executes PowerShell
author: CD_R0M_
description: Detects the abuse of custom file open handler, executing powershell
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*shell\\open\\command\\*" and (Details:"*powershell*" and Details:"*-command*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Registry Persistence Attempt Via DbgManagedDebugger
author: frack113
description: Detects the addition of the "Debugger" value to the "DbgManagedDebugger" key in order to achieve persistence. Which will get invoked when an application crashes
false_positives: Legitimate use of the key to setup a debugger. Which is often the case on developers machines
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\.NETFramework\\DbgManagedDebugger" and (not Details:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\vsjitdebugger.exe\" PID %d APPDOM %d EXTEXT \"%s\" EVTHDL %d"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Exclusions Added - Registry
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the Setting of Windows Defender Exclusions
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Exclusions*"

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Via Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Stephen Lincoln @slincoln-aiq (AttackIQ)
description:
Detects registry value settings that would replace the user's desktop background.
This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.
false_positives: Administrative scripts that change the desktop background to a company logo or other image.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*Control Panel\\Desktop*", "*CurrentVersion\\Policies\\ActiveDesktop*", "*CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System*")) and ((TargetObject:"*NoChangingWallpaper" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)") or TargetObject:"*\\Wallpaper" or (TargetObject:"*\\WallpaperStyle" and Details:"2")) and (not (Image:"*\\svchost.exe" or (TargetObject:"*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\Wallpaper" and Details:"(Empty)") or Image:"*C:\\Windows\\Explorer.EXE")) and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Amazon\\EC2Launch\\EC2Launch.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Amazon\\EC2Launch\\EC2Launch.exe")) and TargetObject:"*\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\Wallpaper")))

name: SIGMA - Antivirus Filter Driver Disallowed On Dev Drive - Registry
author: @kostastsale, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects activity that indicates a user disabling the ability for Antivirus mini filter to inspect a "Dev Drive".
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\FilterManager\\FltmgrDevDriveAllowAntivirusFilter" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Hypervisor Enforced Code Integrity Disabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Anish Bogati
description: Detects changes to the HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity registry key and the "Enabled" value being set to 0 in order to disable the Hypervisor Enforced Code Integrity feature. This allows an attacker to load unsigned and untrusted code to be run in the kernel
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\DeviceGuard\\HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity", "*\\Control\\DeviceGuard\\HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity", "*\\Control\\DeviceGuard\\Scenarios\\HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity\\Enabled")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Hypervisor Enforced Paging Translation Disabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the "DisableHypervisorEnforcedPagingTranslation" registry value. Where the it is set to "1" in order to disable the Hypervisor Enforced Paging Translation feature.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\DisableHypervisorEnforcedPagingTranslation" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - DHCP Callout DLL Installation
author: Dimitrios Slamaris
description: Detects the installation of a Callout DLL via CalloutDlls and CalloutEnabled parameter in Registry, which can be used to execute code in context of the DHCP server (restart required)
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Services\\DHCPServer\\Parameters\\CalloutDlls", "*\\Services\\DHCPServer\\Parameters\\CalloutEnabled"))

name: SIGMA - Disable Exploit Guard Network Protection on Windows Defender
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects disabling Windows Defender Exploit Guard Network Protection
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender Security Center\\App and Browser protection\\DisallowExploitProtectionOverride*" and Details:"DWORD (00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Disabled Windows Defender Eventlog
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the disabling of the Windows Defender eventlog as seen in relation to Lockbit 3.0 infections
false_positives: Other Antivirus software installations could cause Windows to disable that eventlog (unknown)
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational\\Enabled*" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Disable PUA Protection on Windows Defender
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects disabling Windows Defender PUA protection
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\PUAProtection*" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Disable Tamper Protection on Windows Defender
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects disabling Windows Defender Tamper Protection
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Features\\TamperProtection*" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)") and (not ((Image:"C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*" and Image:"*\\MsMpEng.exe") or Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\MsMpEng.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Disable Administrative Share Creation at Startup
author: frack113
description: Administrative shares are hidden network shares created by Microsoft Windows NT operating systems that grant system administrators remote access to every disk volume on a network-connected system
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Services\\LanmanServer\\Parameters\\*" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\AutoShareWks", "*\\AutoShareServer")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Potential AutoLogger Sessions Tampering
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects tampering with autologger trace sessions which is a technique used by attackers to disable logging
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\WMI\\Autologger\\*" and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\EventLog-*", "*\\Defender*")) and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Enable", "*\\Start")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")) and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\wevtutil.exe" or ((Image like~ ("C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Defender\\*")) and Image:"*\\MsMpEng.exe" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\DefenderApiLogger\\*", "*\\DefenderAuditLogger\\*"))))))

name: SIGMA - Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may disable or modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Services\\SharedAccess\\Parameters\\FirewallPolicy\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\EnableFirewall" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Disable Internal Tools or Feature in Registry
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), CrimpSec
description: Detects registry modifications that change features of internal Windows tools (malware like Agent Tesla uses this technique)
query: any where EventID:13 and (((TargetObject like~ ("*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\DisableCMD", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\NoControlPanel", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\NoRun", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\StartMenuLogOff", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\DisableChangePassword", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\DisableLockWorkstation", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\DisableRegistryTools", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\DisableTaskmgr", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\NoDispBackgroundPage", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\NoDispCPL", "*SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Explorer\\DisableNotificationCenter", "*SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\System\\DisableCMD")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)") or ((TargetObject like~ ("*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin", "*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\InactivityTimeoutSecs", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\shutdownwithoutlogon", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\PushNotifications\\ToastEnabled", "*SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Storage\\Write Protection", "*SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\StorageDevicePolicies\\WriteProtect")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)"))

name: SIGMA - Disable Macro Runtime Scan Scope
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects tampering with the MacroRuntimeScanScope registry key to disable runtime scanning of enabled macros
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Office\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\Common\\Security*") and TargetObject:"*\\MacroRuntimeScanScope" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Disable Privacy Settings Experience in Registry
author: frack113
description: Detects registry modifications that disable Privacy Settings Experience
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\OOBE\\DisablePrivacyExperience" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Disable Windows Security Center Notifications
author: frack113
description: Detect set UseActionCenterExperience to 0 to disable the Windows security center notification
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*Windows\\CurrentVersion\\ImmersiveShell\\UseActionCenterExperience" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Registry Disable System Restore
author: frack113
description: Detects the modification of the registry to disable a system restore on the computer
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\SystemRestore*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\SystemRestore*")) and (TargetObject like~ ("*DisableConfig", "*DisableSR")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Service Disabled - Registry
author: Ján Trenčanský, frack113, AlertIQ, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects when an attacker or tool disables the  Windows Defender service (WinDefend) via the registry
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Services\\WinDefend\\Start" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000004)")

name: SIGMA - Windows Event Log Access Tampering Via Registry
author: X__Junior
description: Detects changes to the Windows EventLog channel permission values. It focuses on changes to the Security Descriptor Definition Language (SDDL) string, as modifications to these values can restrict access to specific users or groups, potentially aiding in defense evasion by controlling who can view or modify a event log channel. Upon execution, the user shouldn't be able to access the event log channel via the event viewer or via utilities such as "Get-EventLog" or "wevtutil".
query: any where EventID:13 and (((TargetObject:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\EventLog\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\CustomSD") or ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\EventLog\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels*")) and TargetObject:"*\\ChannelAccess")) and (Details:"*D:(D;*" or (Details:"*D:(*" and Details:"*)(D;*")) and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\servicing\\TrustedInstaller.exe" or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*" and Image:"*\\TiWorker.exe"))) and (not (Image=="" or (?Image == null))))

name: SIGMA - Disable Windows Firewall by Registry
author: frack113
description: Detect set EnableFirewall to 0 to disable the Windows firewall
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\WindowsFirewall\\StandardProfile\\EnableFirewall", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\WindowsFirewall\\DomainProfile\\EnableFirewall")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Disable Windows Event Logging Via Registry
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects tampering with the "Enabled" registry key in order to disable Windows logging of a Windows event channel
false_positives: Rare falsepositives may occur from legitimate administrators disabling specific event log for troubleshooting
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\Enabled" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)") and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\wevtutil.exe" or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\winsxs\\*" and Image:"*\\TiWorker.exe") or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-FileInfoMinifilter*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-ASN1\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-AppCompat\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-Runtime\\Error\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-CAPI2/Operational\\*"))) or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\servicing\\TrustedInstaller.exe" and TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-Compat-Appraiser*"))) and (not (Image=="" or (?Image == null))))

name: SIGMA - Add DisallowRun Execution to Registry
author: frack113
description: Detect set DisallowRun to 1 to prevent user running specific computer program
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\DisallowRun" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Persistence Via Disk Cleanup Handler - Autorun
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects when an attacker modifies values of the Disk Cleanup Handler in the registry to achieve persistence via autorun.
The disk cleanup manager is part of the operating system.
It displays the dialog box […] The user has the option of enabling or disabling individual handlers by selecting or clearing their check box in the disk cleanup manager's UI.
Although Windows comes with a number of disk cleanup handlers, they aren't designed to handle files produced by other applications.
Instead, the disk cleanup manager is designed to be flexible and extensible by enabling any developer to implement and register their own disk cleanup handler.
Any developer can extend the available disk cleanup services by implementing and registering a disk cleanup handler.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\VolumeCaches\\*" and ((TargetObject:"*\\Autorun*" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)") or ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\CleanupString*", "*\\PreCleanupString*")) and (Details like~ ("*cmd*", "*powershell*", "*rundll32*", "*mshta*", "*cscript*", "*wscript*", "*wsl*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\TEMP\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - DNS-over-HTTPS Enabled by Registry
author: Austin Songer
description:
Detects when a user enables DNS-over-HTTPS.
This can be used to hide internet activity or be used to hide the process of exfiltrating data.
With this enabled organization will lose visibility into data such as query type, response and originating IP that are used to determine bad actors.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Edge\\BuiltInDnsClientEnabled" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)") or (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Google\\Chrome\\DnsOverHttpsMode" and Details:"secure") or (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\DNSOverHTTPS\\Enabled" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)"))

name: SIGMA - New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the installation of a DNS plugin DLL via ServerLevelPluginDll parameter in registry, which can be used to execute code in context of the DNS server (restart required)
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\services\\DNS\\Parameters\\ServerLevelPluginDll"

name: SIGMA - ETW Logging Disabled In .NET Processes - Sysmon Registry
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Potential adversaries stopping ETW providers recording loaded .NET assemblies.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\.NETFramework\\ETWEnabled" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)") or ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\COMPlus_ETWEnabled", "*\\COMPlus_ETWFlags")) and (Details like~ (0, "DWORD (0x00000000)"))))

name: SIGMA - Directory Service Restore Mode(DSRM) Registry Value Tampering
author: Nischal Khadgi
description:
Detects changes to "DsrmAdminLogonBehavior" registry value.
During a Domain Controller (DC) promotion, administrators create a Directory Services Restore Mode (DSRM) local administrator account with a password that rarely changes. The DSRM account is an “Administrator” account that logs in with the DSRM mode when the server is booting up to restore AD backups or recover the server from a failure.
Attackers could abuse DSRM account to maintain their persistence and access to the organization's Active Directory.
If the "DsrmAdminLogonBehavior" value is set to "0", the administrator account can only be used if the DC starts in DSRM.
If the "DsrmAdminLogonBehavior" value is set to "1", the administrator account can only be used if the local AD DS service is stopped.
If the "DsrmAdminLogonBehavior" value is set to "2", the administrator account can always be used.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Lsa\\DsrmAdminLogonBehavior" and (not Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)"))

name: SIGMA - Periodic Backup For System Registry Hives Enabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the enabling of the "EnablePeriodicBackup" registry value. Once enabled, The OS will backup System registry hives on restarts to the "C:\Windows\System32\config\RegBack" folder. Windows creates a "RegIdleBackup" task to manage subsequent backups.
Registry backup was a default behavior on Windows and was disabled as of "Windows 10, version 1803".
false_positives: Legitimate need for RegBack feature by administrators.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Session Manager\\Configuration Manager\\EnablePeriodicBackup" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Windows Recall Feature Enabled - Registry
author: Sajid Nawaz Khan
description:
Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature via registry manipulation. Windows Recall can be enabled by setting the value of "DisableAIDataAnalysis" to "0".
Adversaries may enable Windows Recall as part of post-exploitation discovery and collection activities.
This rule assumes that Recall is already explicitly disabled on the host, and subsequently enabled by the adversary.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\WindowsAI\\DisableAIDataAnalysis" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Enabling COR Profiler Environment Variables
author: Jose Rodriguez (@Cyb3rPandaH), OTR (Open Threat Research), Jimmy Bayne (@bohops)
description: Detects .NET Framework CLR and .NET Core CLR "cor_enable_profiling" and "cor_profiler" variables being set and configured.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\COR_ENABLE_PROFILING", "*\\COR_PROFILER", "*\\CORECLR_ENABLE_PROFILING")) or TargetObject:"*\\CORECLR_PROFILER_PATH*")

name: SIGMA - Scripted Diagnostics Turn Off Check Enabled - Registry
author: Christopher Peacock @securepeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: Detects enabling TurnOffCheck which can be used to bypass defense of MSDT Follina vulnerability
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\ScriptedDiagnostics\\TurnOffCheck" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)")

name: SIGMA - Potential EventLog File Location Tampering
author: D3F7A5105
description: Detects tampering with EventLog service "file" key. In order to change the default location of an Evtx file. This technique is used to tamper with log collection and alerting
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\EventLog\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\File") and (not Details:"*\\System32\\Winevt\\Logs\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Application Allowed Through Exploit Guard
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects applications being added to the "allowed applications" list of exploit guard in order to bypass controlled folder settings
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Windows Defender Exploit Guard\\Controlled Folder Access\\AllowedApplications*" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Change User Account Associated with the FAX Service
author: frack113
description: Detect change of the user account associated with the FAX service to avoid the escalation problem.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\Fax\\ObjectName" and (not Details:"*NetworkService*"))

name: SIGMA - Change the Fax Dll
author: frack113
description: Detect possible persistence using Fax DLL load when service restart
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Fax\\Device Providers\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\ImageName*") and (not Details:"%systemroot%\\system32\\fxst30.dll"))

name: SIGMA - FileFix - Command Evidence in TypedPaths from Browser File Upload Abuse
author: Alfie Champion (delivr.to)
description: Detects commonly-used chained commands and strings in the most recent 'url' value of the 'TypedPaths' key, which could be indicative of a user being targeted by the FileFix technique.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\TypedPaths\\url1" and Details:"*#*" and (Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\firefox.exe", "*\\msedge.exe"))) and (Details like~ ("*cmd*", "*curl*", "*powershell*", "*bitsadmin*", "*certutil*", "*mshta*", "*regsvr32*")))

name: SIGMA - New File Association Using Exefile
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Detects the abuse of the exefile handler in new file association. Used for bypass of security products.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*Classes\\.*" and Details:"exefile")

name: SIGMA - Add Debugger Entry To Hangs Key For Persistence
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker adds a new "Debugger" value to the "Hangs" key in order to achieve persistence which will get invoked when an application crashes
false_positives: This value is not set by default but could be rarly used by administrators
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Windows Error Reporting\\Hangs\\Debugger*"

name: SIGMA - Persistence Via Hhctrl.ocx
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker modifies the registry value of the "hhctrl" to point to a custom binary
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\CLSID\\{52A2AAAE-085D-4187-97EA-8C30DB990436}\\InprocServer32\\(Default)*" and (not Details:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\hhctrl.ocx"))

name: SIGMA - Registry Modification to Hidden File Extension
author: frack113
description: Hides the file extension through modification of the registry
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Advanced\\HideFileExt" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)") or (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Advanced\\Hidden" and Details:"DWORD (0x00000002)"))

name: SIGMA - Displaying Hidden Files Feature Disabled
author: frack113
description:
Detects modifications to the "Hidden" and "ShowSuperHidden" explorer registry values in order to disable showing of hidden files and system files.
This technique is abused by several malware families to hide their files from normal users.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Advanced\\ShowSuperHidden", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Advanced\\Hidden")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Registry Hide Function from User
author: frack113
description: Detects registry modifications that hide internal tools or functions from the user (malware like Agent Tesla, Hermetic Wiper uses this technique)
query: any where EventID:13 and (((TargetObject like~ ("*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\HideClock", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\HideSCAHealth", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\HideSCANetwork", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\HideSCAPower", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\HideSCAVolume")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000001)") or ((TargetObject like~ ("*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Advanced\\ShowInfoTip", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Advanced\\ShowCompColor")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)"))

name: SIGMA - Hide Schedule Task Via Index Value Tamper
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects when the "index" value of a scheduled task is modified from the registry
Which effectively hides it from any tooling such as "schtasks /query" (Read the referenced link for more information about the effects of this technique)
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Schedule\\TaskCache\\Tree\\*" and TargetObject:"*Index*") and Details:"DWORD (0x00000000)")

name: SIGMA - Driver Added To Disallowed Images In HVCI - Registry
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Omar Khaled (@beacon_exe)
description: Detects changes to the "HVCIDisallowedImages" registry value to potentially add a driver to the list, in order to prevent it from loading.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of this key would also trigger this. Investigate the driver being added and make sure its intended
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Control\\CI\\*" and TargetObject:"*\\HVCIDisallowedImages*")

name: SIGMA - IE ZoneMap Setting Downgraded To MyComputer Zone For HTTP Protocols
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag (idea)
description: Detects changes to Internet Explorer's (IE / Windows Internet properties) ZoneMap configuration of the "HTTP" and "HTTPS" protocols to point to the "My Computer" zone. This allows downloaded files from the Internet to be granted the same level of trust as files stored locally.
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Internet Settings\\ZoneMap\\ProtocolDefaults*" and (TargetObject like~ ("*\\http", "*\\https")) and Details:"*DWORD (0x00000000)*")

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Extension In Keyboard Layout IME File Registry Value
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of Windows Input Method Editor (IME) keyboard layout feature, which allows an attacker to load a DLL into the process after sending the WM_INPUTLANGCHANGEREQUEST message.
Before doing this, the client needs to register the DLL in a special registry key that is assumed to implement this keyboard layout. This registry key should store a value named "Ime File" with a DLL path.
IMEs are essential for languages that have more characters than can be represented on a standard keyboard, such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
false_positives: IMEs are essential for languages that have more characters than can be represented on a standard keyboard, such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Keyboard Layouts\\*" and TargetObject:"*Ime File*") and (not Details:"*.ime"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Path In Keyboard Layout IME File Registry Value
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of Windows Input Method Editor (IME) keyboard layout feature, which allows an attacker to load a DLL into the process after sending the WM_INPUTLANGCHANGEREQUEST message.
Before doing this, the client needs to register the DLL in a special registry key that is assumed to implement this keyboard layout. This registry key should store a value named "Ime File" with a DLL path.
IMEs are essential for languages that have more characters than can be represented on a standard keyboard, such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Control\\Keyboard Layouts\\*" and TargetObject:"*Ime File*") and ((Details like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*")) or ((Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Contacts\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - New Root or CA or AuthRoot Certificate to Store
author: frack113
description: Detects the addition of new root, CA or AuthRoot certificates to the Windows registry
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\SystemCertificates\\Root\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\SystemCertificates\\Root\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\EnterpriseCertificates\\Root\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\SystemCertificates\\CA\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\SystemCertificates\\CA\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\EnterpriseCertificates\\CA\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\SystemCertificates\\AuthRoot\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\SystemCertificates\\AuthRoot\\Certificates\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\EnterpriseCertificates\\AuthRoot\\Certificates\\*")) and TargetObject:"*\\Blob" and Details:"Binary Data")

name: SIGMA - Internet Explorer DisableFirstRunCustomize Enabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the Internet Explorer "DisableFirstRunCustomize" value, which prevents Internet Explorer from running the first run wizard the first time a user starts the browser after installing Internet Explorer or Windows.
false_positives: As this is controlled by group policy as well as user settings. Some false positives may occur.
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\Microsoft\\Internet Explorer\\Main\\DisableFirstRunCustomize" and (Details like~ ("DWORD (0x00000001)", "DWORD (0x00000002)"))) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\explorer.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\ie4uinit.exe"))) and (not (((Image:"*\\Temp\\*" and Image:"*\\.cr\\avira_*") and Details:"*DWORD (0x00000001)*") or ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Foxit Software\\Foxit PDF Reader\\FoxitPDFReader.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Foxit Software\\Foxit PDF Reader\\FoxitPDFReader.exe")) and Details:"*DWORD (0x00000001)*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Ransomware Activity Using LegalNotice Message
author: frack113
description: Detect changes to the "LegalNoticeCaption" or "LegalNoticeText" registry values where the message set contains keywords often used in ransomware ransom messages
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\LegalNoticeCaption*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\LegalNoticeText*")) and (Details like~ ("*encrypted*", "*Unlock-Password*", "*paying*")))

name: SIGMA - Lolbas OneDriveStandaloneUpdater.exe Proxy Download
author: frack113
description:
Detects setting a custom URL for OneDriveStandaloneUpdater.exe to download a file from the Internet without executing any
anomalous executables with suspicious arguments. The downloaded file will be in C:\Users\redacted\AppData\Local\Microsoft\OneDrive\StandaloneUpdaterreSignInSettingsConfig.json
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\OneDrive\\UpdateOfficeConfig\\UpdateRingSettingURLFromOC*"

name: SIGMA - Lsass Full Dump Request Via DumpType Registry Settings
author: @pbssubhash
description: Detects the setting of the "DumpType" registry value to "2" which stands for a "Full Dump". Technique such as LSASS Shtinkering requires this value to be "2" in order to dump LSASS.
false_positives: Legitimate application that needs to do a full dump of their process
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Windows Error Reporting\\LocalDumps\\DumpType*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Windows Error Reporting\\LocalDumps\\lsass.exe\\DumpType*")) and Details:"DWORD (0x00000002)")

name: SIGMA - RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering
author: frack113
description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa\\DisableRestrictedAdmin"

name: SIGMA - Blue Mockingbird - Registry
author: Trent Liffick (@tliffick)
description: Attempts to detect system changes made by Blue Mockingbird
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\wercplsupport\\Parameters\\ServiceDll"

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Netsh Helper DLL - Registry
author: Anish Bogati
description: Detects changes to the Netsh registry key to add a new DLL value. This change might be an indication of a potential persistence attempt by adding a malicious Netsh helper
false_positives: Legitimate helper added by different programs and the OS
query: any where EventID:13 and ((TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\NetSh*" and Details:"*.dll*") and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\poqexec.exe" and (Details like~ ("ipmontr.dll", "iasmontr.dll", "ippromon.dll")))))

name: SIGMA - New Netsh Helper DLL Registered From A Suspicious Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the Netsh registry key to add a new DLL value that is located on a suspicious location. This change might be an indication of a potential persistence attempt by adding a malicious Netsh helper
query: any where EventID:13 and (TargetObject:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\NetSh*" and ((Details like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*")) or ((Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Contacts\\*") or (Details:"*:\\Users\\*" and Details:"*\\Pictures\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - NET NGenAssemblyUsageLog Registry Key Tamper
author: frack113
description:
Detects changes to the NGenAssemblyUsageLog registry key.
.NET Usage Log output location can be controlled by setting the NGenAssemblyUsageLog CLR configuration knob in the Registry or by configuring an environment variable (as described in the next section).
By simplify specifying an arbitrary value (e.g. fake output location or junk data) for the expected value, a Usage Log file for the .NET execution context will not be created.
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\.NETFramework\\NGenAssemblyUsageLog"

name: SIGMA - New Application in AppCompat
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: A General detection for a new application in AppCompat. This indicates an application executing for the first time on an endpoint.
false_positives: This rule is to explore new applications on an endpoint. False positives depends on the organization.
query: any where EventID:13 and TargetObject:"*\\AppCompatFlags\\Compatibility Assistant\\Store\\*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - REG
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
false_positives: Other legitimate network providers used and not filtred in this rule
